domain: Inuit qajaq (Arctic skin-on-frame kayak; steamed C-shape ribs act as per-frame springs lashed by seal sinew; each frame absorbs wave shock independently; depth-graded outer-stronger; skin tensioned to shrink-fit)
specific_mechanism: Per-frame independent elastic shock dampening — each rib absorbs and releases wave energy locally; the lashing allows joints to slip, so shocks dampen layer-by-layer without rigid transmission to neighbouring ribs; outer ribs (near gunwale) absorb more.
llm_application: QAJAQ-DAMPER: a per-layer learnable elastic-dampening coefficient α_ℓ ∈ [0,1] that scales gradient propagation between layers as a function of gradient-norm spike detection. Each Transformer layer carries (i) a spike-detector (gradient-norm above 95th-percentile rolling-window threshold triggers active dampening) AND (ii) a depth-graded prior anti-correlated with depth (outer layers default α_outer=0.5 vs inner α_inner=0.9 preserve signal). When a spike is detected at layer ℓ, the gradient flowing to layer ℓ−1 is scaled by α_ℓ (lashed-joint slip) for the duration of the spike-window. The coefficient α_ℓ is learnable per layer.
novelty_claim: Standard gradient-clipping is global (clip-by-norm). AdaGrad/Adam/AdaFactor adapt per-parameter learning rates. GPAS scales activation variance while preserving gradient. Progressive Layer Dropping randomly drops layers. Spike-No-More controls sub-layer:shortcut ratio statically. QAJAQ-DAMPER differs by combining (1) PER-LAYER LEARNABLE COEFFICIENT, (2) SPIKE-DETECTOR triggered, (3) DEPTH-GRADED prior anti-correlated with depth, (4) GRADIENT-PROPAGATION SCALING (not activation, not LR). The combination is the lashed-rib analogy.
